title: nothopegia colebrookeana
intro: Nothopegia colebrookeana, also known as Colebrooke's Calophyllum, is a species of evergreen tree in the family Calophyllaceae, native to the tropical forests of Sri Lanka. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, with its bark, seeds, and leaves exhibiting medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Nothopegia colebrookeana include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as a treatment for digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the bark and seeds of Nothopegia colebrookeana can be decocted or made into a paste for topical application, while the leaves can be used to make a medicinal tea.
